item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this one. A simple and quick yet interesting read. I noticed some people don't like that the character is one-dimensional but I think that fits very well because that's the point. He's supposed to be the embodiment of fighting crime so it makes sense for him to be all-good and bent on stopping evil-doers. After all when the criminals are put down he himself disappears because his job is done. To me the Specter is like the Batman of the SCP universe and I like it. It's refreshing to see an SCP that isn't evil or causes terrible effects.

The only thing not to my taste is the conversation between the foundation agents and the SCP where it starts using slang and talk about video games. I feel like it would fit better if he sounded more like a professional and less like a teenager.

I recommend reading the article with The Dark Knight Main Theme playing in the background, it's pretty epic.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Obligatory music link

Jokes aside, This was pretty good so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you get it because it's gone completely over my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ahhh！I think the Specter is a little lovable.give you my +1
and

SCP-4494: Yeah, and as the embodiment of justice let me tell you, that new Spider-Man game? Fucking good stuff! You played it?

Is the Specter's prototype come from Spider-Man Noir?cool.I really like him!XDD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s good and now I what to hear the backstory to this scp

+1

item.title: SCP-4494
item.content:encoded: Exactly what I thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see why not? It adds a unique element to this SCP and the dramatic whisper may be a reference to legends the like of which can only be spoken in hushed whisper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who is this nerd clad in black I love him

Take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like this and I don't think it takes the real world implications of what crime is into account. - 1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice, it's not only cool to see another SCP use a "Special" class but this one uses it in a nice way that hasn't been done before. I like that it's the embodiment of justice though I feel like this could possibly be a "minor Thaumiel" if it appeared to assist in containing other anomalies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What font did you use for "the Specter"? was it custom or is it already in the wiki. How did you download the font onto your article if it was custom?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Additionally, it possesses minor infohazardous properties; when referred to in text the reference will always appear in a stylistic and dramatic font, and speakers invariably use a dramatic whisper when referring to SCP-4494 verbally.

What is the point of this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He lives in society.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the specter likes to fight crime and play video games so much, why doesn't he attack EA for its criminal microtransactions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True, maybe trying to amplify its effects to remove the concept of criminal activity? that would make for a good tale. But probably a job for the ethics committee than anything else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about you, but SCP-4494 seems like a sort of guy the Foundation can exploit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nvm. I can't read.

The concept is very good along with the delivery. Like the font too. Brings that comic book nostalgia.

+1
We did it folks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The original image is linked in my author post at the top

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that image literally just photo shopped Rorschach? That mask seems eerily familiar

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having to post this since someone edited yesterday and I didn't notice, which functionally broke some of the interview, so:

PLEASE DO NOT CHANGE THE FORMATTING ON THE TEXT IN THE INTERVIEW, ITS INTENTIONAL

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea isn’t quite strong enough to carry this one for me. I feel like it might have been more interesting if the way society breaks down had been more of a focus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've made the font a bit bigger so hopefully that will help that a little.

Also, how did we come to the conclusion that this is the literal, physical embodiment of crime-fighting?

My headcanon is that the metaphysics department keeps on top of stuff like this pretty well. The first time it went wrong they would have noticed something was off and worked it out from there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This image is really cool and all but that font when we refer to its designation can get really annoying to look at sometimes. Also, how did we come to the conclusion that this is the literal, physical embodiment of crime-fighting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: very clever …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept of the people in a geographic area losing their sense of right and wrong isn't explained very well and the sentence "People within this area no longer conceptualize the belief that crime is something which should be stopped" feels like it's begging for greater depth in its criteria for how different people perceive crime.

I also don't think it was well-explained why losing the sense of criminal consequences would lead people to riot. Is it a statement on the human condition, or is the escalation also a part of the anomalous effect?

The consequences of this being a jamcon article; being in this time zone and having to do this after work doesn't leave much room to work with - had about 4 hours to get this written, the picture photoshoped, critted, fixed up and ready for posting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just can't take this seriously as a mainlist article. It's way too goofy while also trying to be self-serious and the text alterations feel over-the-top ridiculous. Were this a -J it would be amusing but as it stands I can't take this seriously as written.

The concept of the people in a geographic area losing their sense of right and wrong isn't explained very well and the sentence "People within this area no longer conceptualize the belief that crime is something which should be stopped" feels like it's begging for greater depth in its criteria for how different people perceive crime.

I also don't think it was well-explained why losing the sense of criminal consequences would lead people to riot. Is it a statement on the human condition, or is the escalation also a part of the anomalous effect?

The Archon psuedo-object class feels more like an excuse to skip out on the special containment procedures than something which adds anything to the narrative.

Also, the think that I think drags this down more than anything is that the Spectre is just not an interesting character as presented here. He's very one-dimensional.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is silly and i love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not broken - intentional ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dramatic font is broken near the end of the interview log.

All in all I really enjoyed this, I wonder what would happen though if this entity was thrown into an environment under a very different set of laws, or a place where there's no legal authority.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is perfectly written to provide an obvious sense of scp weirdness but also a perfect amount of comic relief and humor. +1!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
PLEASE DO NOT CHANGE THE FORMATTING ON THE TEXT IN THE INTERVIEW, ITS INTENTIONAL

Okay, here's my jam-con 2019 entry for the Pulp Fiction theme!

Thanks to CaptainKirby, Rounderhouse, weryillium, MaliceAF, Laneous and not_a_seagull for reading the draft!

Image is taken here here and then edited to largely black out the colour and detail on the guy https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shadow_Cosplay_Photograph_from_Comic-Con_International_San_Diego.jpg

Find more of my stuff here!
